mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during documented rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and impending economic slowdown, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand strength, driving earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series in multivariate regression, or positive return frequency falls below 45% when both series rise concurrently over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month expansion in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with a t-statistic below 1.5 across 100 independent observations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive federal funds rates above 2.5% and rising unemployment above 3.5% indicates monetary policy is overheating the labor market, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns exceed 50% when both conditions hold simultaneously, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample predictive regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surpassing 2.0% confirms robust macroeconomic expansion, supporting corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 40% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal exhibits negative autocorrelation in walk-forward validation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index readings trigger immediate expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases fail to predict negative 5-day returns, or positive return frequency exceeds 55% during documented inflation spikes across a 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect healthy labor market absorption and rising consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll gains fail to correlate with positive equity returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance at 50% in out-of-sample tests.
